summary.summary: The dominant sequence transduction models are based on complex recurrent or convolutional neural networks that include an encoder and a decoder. We propose a new simple network architecture, the Transformer, based solely on attention mechanisms. The model outperforms the best previously reported models including ensembles by more than 2.0 BLEU.
chunk_summaries: The dominant sequence transduction models are based on complex recurrent or convolutional neural networks that include an encoder and a decoder. We propose a new simple network architecture, the Transformer, based solely on attention mechanisms. The model outperforms the best previously reported models including ensembles by more than 2.0 BLEU.

references: Bahdanau, D., Cho, K., Bengio, Y. Neural machine translation by jointly learning to align and translate. ICLR 2015.
Hochreiter, S., Schmidhuber, J. Long short-term memory. Neural Computation, 1997.
Sutskever, I., Vinyals, O., Le, Q. Sequence to sequence learning with neural networks. NeurIPS 2014.
Cho, K., et al. Learning phrase representations using RNN encoder-decoder. EMNLP 2014.
Devlin, J., Chang, M., Lee, K., Toutanova, K. BERT: Pre-training of deep bidirectional transformers. NAACL 2019.

methodology.methodology_excerpt:
approaches in
sequence modeling. Numerous efforts have since continued to push the boundaries of recurrent
language models and encoder-decoder architectures.

Methodology: We trained on the standard WMT 2014 English-German dataset consisting of
about 4.5 million sentence pairs. We used the Adam optimizer with beta1=0.9, beta2=0.98.
We used multi-head attention with 8 heads. The model was evaluated using BLEU score.
The Transformer uses BERT-style embeddings and ResNet skip connections for stabil...

limitations: No explicit limitations section detected in this paper.
future_directions:
We are excited about the future of attention-based models and
plan to apply them to other problems.
